title: Effect Service & Tool Permission Patterns — OpenCode Codebase

sections.heading: 1. Context.Service Pattern
2. Effect.fn() Pattern
3. Layer.effect() Structure
4. LLM.StreamInput Type
sections.body:
Defined at session/llm.ts:33-46

```ts
export type StreamInput = {
  user: MessageV2.User                        // The user message that triggered this stream
  sessionID: string                           // Current session ID
  parentSessionID?: string                     // Parent session (for forked sessions)
  model: Provider.Model                        // The model configuration
  agent: Agent.Info                            // The agent configuration (includes permission ruleset)
  permission?: Permission.Ruleset              // Session-level permission overrides (optional)
  system: string[]                             // System prompt strings
  messages: ModelMessage[]                     // AI SDK message array (conversation history)
  small?: boolean                              // If true, reduce output tokens (for quick responses)
  tools: Record<string, Tool>                  // AI SDK Tool map (from `ai` package)
  retries?: number                             // Max retries (default 0)
  toolChoice?: "auto" | "required" | "none"    // Tool choice strategy
}
```

=== Key field: `tools: Record<string, Tool>` ===
This is the AI SDK's `Tool` type (imported as `type Tool as AITool from "ai"`), not the codebase's `Tool.Info`. The `ai` SDK's `Tool` interface:
```ts
interface Tool {
  description: string
  parameters: JSONSchema7
  execute?: (args: any, options: ToolExecutionOptions) => Promise<...>
}
```

=== Key field: `permission?: Permission.Ruleset` ===
Optional session-level permission rules that are merged with agent permissions when evaluating tool access. This is how sessions can override agent defaults.

=== Construction in tests (processor-effect.test.ts:244) ===
```ts
const input = {
  user: { ... } satisfies MessageV2.User,
  sessionID: chat.id,
  model: mdl,
  agent: agent(),  // Agent.Info with permission array
  system: [],
  messages: [{ role: "user", content: "hi" }],
  tools: {
    lookup: tool({
      description: "Look up information",
      inputSchema: z.object({ query: z.string() }),
      execute: async (input) => ({
        title: "Weather lookup",
        output: `result:${input.query}`,
        metadata: { source: "test" },
      }),
    }),
  },
} satisfies LLM.StreamInput
```

sections.heading: 5. Permission System
sections.body:
=== Permission.Rule (permission/index.ts:22) ===
```ts
export const Rule = Schema.Struct({
  permission: Schema.String,    // Tool permission name (e.g. "edit", "read", "*", "bash")
  pattern: Schema.String,       // Glob pattern (e.g. "*", "src/**")
  action: Action,               // "allow" | "deny" | "ask"
})
// Action = "allow" | "deny" | "ask"

export type Ruleset = Rule[]  // Schema.Array(Rule)
```

=== Permission.Interface (permission/index.ts:122) ===
```ts
export interface Interface {
  readonly ask: (input: AskInput) => Effect.Effect<void, Error>
  readonly reply: (input: ReplyInput) => Effect.Effect<void, NotFoundError>
  readonly list: () => Effect.Effect<ReadonlyArray<Request>>
}
```

`Permission.ask()` is the core evaluation function. It:
1. Evaluates each pattern against the ruleset + approved list
2. If any matches a "deny" rule → returns `DeniedError`
3. If all match "allow" rules → returns void immediately
4. Otherwise → publishes `Event.Asked` to bus and waits for user `reply()`

=== Agent.Info (agent/agent.ts:29-49) ===
```ts
export const Info = Schema.Struct({
  name: Schema.String,
  mode: Schema.Literals(["subagent", "primary", "all"]),
  native: Schema.optional(Schema.Boolean),
  permission: Permission.Ruleset,  // Always present on every agent
  // ... other fields
})
```

=== Permission Merging (permission/index.ts:305) ===
```ts
export function merge(...rulesets: Ruleset[]): Rule[] {
  return [...PermissionV2.merge(...rulesets)]
}
```
Later rulesets override earlier ones based on specificity. Used to combine:
- Agent defaults + user config + session overrides
